stem: A small business is choosing between a star topology and a mesh topology for the LAN in their new office.
model_answer: In a star topology every device connects to a central switch; if a single cable fails only that one device is affected, but the whole network depends on the switch. In a mesh every device connects to many others, so there are multiple paths between any two devices: the network keeps working when any single link fails, and traffic can be balanced across paths. Stars are cheaper and simpler to add to; meshes are more resilient and offer higher throughput, but they need far more cabling and are more expensive. For a small office, a star is usually the best choice — predictable cost, easy to manage, switch-failure risk mitigated by keeping a spare.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Award marks for: feature of star, feature of mesh, benefit of one applied to a small office, drawback of one, recommendation tied to office context. 6 marks total.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Compare a star topology and a mesh topology and recommend, with reasons, which is better for the small office.

mark_points.text: Star: each device connects to a central switch / hub
Mesh: each device connects to many others / multiple paths between devices
Benefit: star is cheap and easy to add new devices; mesh is resilient if a single link fails
Drawback: star fails entirely if the switch fails; mesh needs much more cabling
Applies the comparison to a small office (cost / scale / failure tolerance)
Justified recommendation that names the choice
